reasoning: The article describes the peroxidase-mimicking activity of gold-loaded nanoporous ferric oxide nanocubes (Au-NPFe2O3NC) for the development of a molecular sensor with enhanced electrocatalytic and colorimetric detection of autoantibodies. The experiments involve the catalytic oxidation of 3,3',5,5'-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) in the presence of hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) at room temperature. The kinetic parameters, such as Michaelis-Menten constant (Km) and maximum reaction rate (Vmax), are determined for both TMB and H2O2. The experiments are conducted by varying the concentrations of TMB and H2O2 while keeping the other reactant at a fixed concentration. The article provides detailed information on the experimental conditions, including pH, temperature, and the concentrations of the reactants.
